summary: Matched two small Dolphin SDK areas by replacing assembly with C: PADClamp in the pad subsystem and AXFX allocation hook helpers in axfx. The PR removed asm/dolphin/pad/Padclamp.s and asm/dolphin/axfx/axfx.s from the build, added src/dolphin/axfx/axfx.c, extended src/dolphin/pad/PadClamp.c with PADClamp, and moved the __OSCurrHeap declaration into OSAlloc.h so AXFX code could use the OS allocator interface cleanly.
what_changed: Deleted the hand-written assembly file asm/dolphin/pad/Padclamp.s after matching PADClamp in C.

Added PADClamp to src/dolphin/pad/PadClamp.c, iterating over PAD_CHANMAX controller status entries, skipping non-PAD_ERR_NONE entries, clamping main stick and substick via ClampStick, and applying min/max trigger clamping to triggerLeft and triggerRight.
Deleted asm/dolphin/axfx/axfx.s and replaced it with src/dolphin/axfx/axfx.c.
Implemented AXFXAllocFunction, AXFXFreeFunction, AXFXSetHooks, and the __AXFXAlloc/__AXFXFree function pointer globals in C.
AXFXAllocFunction and AXFXFreeFunction now call OSAllocFromHeap(__OSCurrHeap, size) and OSFreeToHeap(__OSCurrHeap, ptr), preserving the assembly behavior through the OS allocator API.
Updated obj_files.mk to remove the pad assembly object and use src/dolphin/axfx/axfx.c.o instead of asm/dolphin/axfx/axfx.s.o.
Changed src/dolphin/os/OSAlloc.h to expose extern volatile OSHeapHandle __OSCurrHeap instead of leaving it commented out.
Removed a local extern OSHeapHandle __OSCurrHeap declaration from src/sysdolphin/baselib/initialize.c, relying on the shared OSAlloc.h declaration instead.
smart_moves: Kept AXFX allocation hooks very close to the original assembly: argument forwarding is simple, __OSCurrHeap is passed as the heap handle, and __AXFXAlloc/__AXFXFree are initialized directly to the default functions.
Reused the existing ClampStick helper and ClampRegion fields rather than duplicating stick math inside PADClamp.
Used PADStatus field names such as stickX, stickY, substickX, substickY, triggerLeft, triggerRight, and err to express the structure offsets that were formerly implicit in assembly.
Centralized the __OSCurrHeap declaration in OSAlloc.h, reducing ad hoc extern declarations and making allocator users include the correct Dolphin OS header.
Updated obj_files.mk alongside source additions/deletions, which is essential for decomp PRs that replace assembly with C objects.
decomp_lessons: Small SDK wrapper functions are good candidates for direct asm-to-C replacement when the assembly is just register shuffling plus a call into an already-decompiled API.
For functions that walk arrays of SDK structs, matching can often be expressed naturally as a for loop over a named constant such as PAD_CHANMAX with pointer increment semantics.
Header cleanup can be part of a matching PR when a newly matched C file needs access to globals previously referenced only from assembly.
When replacing assembly that defines initialized small-data globals, ensure the C file defines the globals with equivalent initializers, as with __AXFXAlloc and __AXFXFree.

Preserve unsigned/signed behavior implied by byte loads and struct fields by using the existing SDK types and named fields where possible; the PR evidence shows PADClamp relies on PADStatus and ClampRegion definitions already present.
naming_conventions: Dolphin SDK exported functions keep their uppercase SDK-style names: PADClamp, AXFXAllocFunction, AXFXFreeFunction, AXFXSetHooks.
Internal AXFX hook globals use double-underscore SDK names: __AXFXAlloc and __AXFXFree.
OS allocator global uses the SDK name __OSCurrHeap and is declared in the relevant OS header.
Pad constants and status fields use existing SDK-style names: PAD_CHANMAX, PAD_ERR_NONE, PADStatus, ClampRegion, ClampStick.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly object entries in obj_files.mk only after adding matching C implementations.
Translated the PADClamp loop from an assembly counter/pointer loop into `for (i = 0; i < PAD_CHANMAX; i++, status++)`.
Translated assembly branch-over behavior for controller errors into `if (status->err != PAD_ERR_NONE) continue;`.
Mapped trigger clamp assembly to a clear two-branch pattern: zero when at or below minTrigger, otherwise cap to maxTrigger then subtract minTrigger.
Matched AXFX sdata function pointer initialization in C by defining `any_t (*__AXFXAlloc)(size_t) = AXFXAllocFunction;` and `void (*__AXFXFree)(any_t) = AXFXFreeFunction;`.
Used a typed allocator hook signature with `any_t` and `size_t`, matching the apparent assembly ABI without introducing extra wrapper logic.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one review recorded, but no review body or line-level feedback was included in the provided context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify PADClamp.c or ClampStick for additional matching or cleanup.
Find later PRs that add a public AXFX header or move AXFX hook declarations out of axfx.c.
Search for remaining ad hoc extern declarations of __OSCurrHeap after OSAlloc.h exposed it.
Check whether other Dolphin SDK assembly wrappers can be matched using the same allocator-hook pattern.
